## [00:00 - 03:01]

Lesson number one, the mastery cycle. When I ran my last workshop, I remember looking out into the audience and I nearly fell off the stage because sitting in the audience was Raj Shaman. So I'm doing live coaching. And in this situation, this would normally scare the out of people, but not this guy. Raj literally leaned over to me right before going on stage. And he said to me, then don't hold back. I want you to coach this. That's humility. That's hunger. And that's the words of someone pursuing greatness. The best don't see feedback as criticism. They see it as oxygen. That's why they can continue to climb higher and higher and higher. This right here is the mastery cycle. And it starts with the first step in green, which is try and apply. The reason it starts with that as the first step is because we live in a culture now where people are obsessed with knowledge consumption. We consume, we consume, we consume more, more content, more content. Our poor organic brains are working against supercomputers that are there to program us to stay on the platform and watch another video. But that statement is missing one word, which then actually makes it true because knowledge is not power. If you just keep consuming knowledge, you just become a library.
